name: add-tests
description: Generate tests for a completed phase.

name: ai-integration-phase
description: Generate an AI-SPEC.md design contract for phases that involve building AI systems. Presents an interactive decision matrix, surfaces domain-specific failure modes and eval criteria, and produces `AI-SPEC.md` with a framework recommendation
examples: gsd-ai-integration-phase              # Wizard for the current phase
gsd-ai-integration-phase 3           # Wizard for a specific phase

name: audit-fix
description: Autonomous audit-to-fix pipeline — runs an audit, classifies findings, fixes auto-fixable issues with test verification, and commits each fix atomically.

flags.description: Classify findings without fixing (shows classification table)

examples: gsd-audit-fix                              # Run audit-uat, fix medium+ issues (max 5)
gsd-audit-fix --severity high             # Only fix high-severity issues
gsd-audit-fix --max 10 --severity all     # Fix up to 10 issues of any severity

name: audit-milestone
description: Verify milestone met its definition of done.

name: audit-uat
description: Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items.

name: autonomous
description: Run all remaining phases autonomously.

flags.description: Start from a specific phase number
Stop after completing a specific phase number
Restrict execution to phase N; lifecycle step is skipped
Route each planning step through `/gsd-plan-review-convergence`; requires `workflow.plan_review_convergence=true`
Replace `AskUserQuestion` prompts with plain numbered lists

examples: gsd-autonomous --to 5              # Run up to and including phase 5
gsd-autonomous --only 4 --converge # Run one phase with plan convergence

name: capture
description: Capture ideas, tasks, notes, and seeds to their appropriate destination. Default mode adds a structured todo; flags route to specialized capture workflows.

flags.description: Zero-friction note — append, list (`--note list`), or promote (`--note promote N`)
Add to the backlog parking lot using 999.x numbering
Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions
List pending todos and select one to work on
List/audit captured seeds, optionally filtered by status (read-only)

examples: gsd-capture "Consider adding dark mode support"   # Add todo
gsd-capture --note promote 3                      # Promote note 3 to todo
gsd-capture --backlog "GraphQL API layer"         # Add to backlog
gsd-capture --seed "Add real-time collaboration when WebSocket infra is in place"
gsd-capture --list                                # Browse and act on todos

name: cleanup
description: Archive accumulated phase directories from completed milestones and prune local branches whose upstream has been deleted.

name: code-review
description: Review source files changed during a phase for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality problems. Use `--fix` to auto-fix findings after review.
examples: gsd-code-review 3 --fix                    # Review then fix Critical + Warning findings
gsd-code-review 3 --fix --all             # Review then fix all findings including Info
gsd-code-review 3 --fix --auto            # Review, fix, and re-review until clean (max 3 iterations)

name: complete-milestone
description: Archive milestone, tag release.

name: config
description: Configure GSD settings interactively — workflow toggles, advanced knobs, integrations, and model profile — with a single consolidated command.

flags.description: Power-user knobs: planning tuning, timeouts, branch templates, cross-AI execution, runtime/output
Third-party API keys, code-review CLI routing, agent-skill injection

examples: gsd-config --integrations          # API keys, review CLI routing, agent skills
gsd-config --profile budget        # Switch to budget profile
gsd-config --profile quality       # Switch to quality profile

name: context
description: Codebase intelligence router — map, graphify, docs, learnings

name: debug
description: Systematic debugging with persistent state.

flags.description: Diagnosis-only mode — investigate without attempting fixes

examples: gsd-debug "Login button not responding on mobile Safari"

name: discuss-phase
description: Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning.

flags.description: Skip area selection — discuss all gray areas interactively (no auto-advance)
Auto-select recommended defaults for all questions
Group questions for batch intake instead of one-by-one
File-based bulk question answering from a prepared answers file
Surface Claude's implementation assumptions about the phase without an interactive session

examples: gsd-discuss-phase 1 --all          # Discuss all gray areas without selection step
gsd-discuss-phase --batch          # Batch mode for current phase
gsd-discuss-phase 3 --assumptions  # Surface Claude's assumptions before planning

name: docs-update
description: Generate or update project documentation verified against the codebase.

name: eval-review
description: Audit an executed AI phase's evaluation coverage and produce an EVAL-REVIEW.md remediation plan. Checks implementation against the `AI-SPEC.md` evaluation plan produced by `/gsd-ai-integration-phase`. Scores each eval dimension as COVERED/P

name: execute-phase
description: Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization, or run a specific wave.
examples: gsd-execute-phase 2 --cross-ai     # Delegate phase 2 to external AI CLI

name: explore
description: Socratic ideation session — guide an idea through probing questions, optionally spawn research, then route output to the right GSD artifact (notes, todos, seeds, research questions, requirements, or a new phase).
examples: gsd-explore authentication strategy  # Explore a specific topic

name: extract-learnings
description: Extract reusable patterns, anti-patterns, and architectural decisions from completed phase work.

flags.description: Extract learnings from all completed phases

examples: gsd-extract-learnings --all                # Extract from all completed phases

name: fast
description: Execute a trivial task inline — no subagents, no planning overhead. For typo fixes, config changes, small refactors, forgotten commits.

name: forensics
description: Post-mortem investigation for failed GSD workflows — diagnoses what went wrong.
examples: gsd-forensics "Phase 3 execution stalled"  # With problem description

name: graphify
description: Build, query, and inspect the project knowledge graph stored in `.planning/graphs/`. Opt-in via `graphify.enabled: true` in `config.json` (see [Configuration Reference](CONFIGURATION.md#graphify-settings)); when disabled, the command prints

flags.description: Build or rebuild the knowledge graph (runs `graphify update .` inline and refreshes `.planning/graphs/`)
Search the graph for a term
Show changes since the last build

examples: gsd-graphify build                 # Build or rebuild the knowledge graph
gsd-graphify query authentication  # Search the graph for a term
gsd-graphify status                # Show freshness and statistics
gsd-graphify diff                  # Show changes since last build

name: health
description: Validate `.planning/` directory integrity. With `--context`, probes the

flags.description: Probe context-window utilization; warns at 60 %, critical at 70 %

name: help
description: Show GSD commands at the tier you ask for. Default fits one screen; `--full` is the complete reference; `<topic>` jumps directly to one section.
examples: gsd-help --brief                   # ~10-line one-liner refresher of top commands
gsd-help --full                    # Complete reference (every command, every flag)
gsd-help --brief <topic>           # Compact scoped lookup — signature + one-line summary

name: ideate
description: Exploration and capture router — explore, sketch, spike, spec, capture

name: import
description: Ingest an external plan file into the GSD planning system with conflict detection against `PROJECT.md` decisions before writing anything.
examples: gsd-import --from /tmp/team-plan.md    # Import and validate an external plan
gsd-import --from-gsd2                # Migrate from GSD-2 back to v1 (current dir)
gsd-import --from-gsd2 --path ~/old-project  # Migrate from a different path

name: ingest-docs
description: Bootstrap or merge a .planning/ setup from existing ADRs, PRDs, SPECs, and docs in a repo. Runs parallel classification (`gsd-doc-classifier`) plus synthesis with precedence rules and cycle detection (`gsd-doc-synthesizer`). Produces a thre
examples: gsd-ingest-docs                            # Scan repo root, auto-detect mode
gsd-ingest-docs docs/                      # Only ingest under docs/

name: manage
description: Management router — config, workspace, workstreams, thread, update, ship, inbox

name: manager
description: Interactive command center for managing multiple phases from one terminal.
examples: gsd-manager --analyze-deps         # Scan ROADMAP phases for dependency relationships before parallel execution

name: map-codebase
description: Analyze existing codebase with parallel mapper agents. Use `--fast` for a quick single-agent scan, or `--query` to search existing intel. First-time brownfield setup should usually start with `/gsd-onboard`, which hands off to this command
examples: gsd-map-codebase                   # Full codebase analysis (4 parallel agents)
gsd-map-codebase auth              # Focus on auth area
gsd-map-codebase --fast            # Quick tech + arch overview (1 agent)
gsd-map-codebase --fast --focus quality  # Quality and code health only
gsd-map-codebase --query authentication  # Search intel for a term

name: mempalace-capture
description: File a phase artifact (`CONTEXT.md`, `PLAN.md`, or `SUMMARY.md`) verbatim into MemPalace and mirror decision facts into its temporal knowledge graph. Uses `mempalace_check_duplicate` before filing, so re-running the same phase is idempotent
examples: gsd-mempalace-capture CONTEXT.md   # File CONTEXT.md → decisions room
gsd-mempalace-capture PLAN.md      # File PLAN.md → planning room
gsd-mempalace-capture SUMMARY.md   # File SUMMARY.md → milestones room

name: mempalace-recall
description: Recall prior decisions, patterns, and surprises from MemPalace into `MEMORY-RECALL.md` before planning. Reads `CONTEXT.md` to derive a search query, runs `mempalace wake-up` + `mempalace_search` + `mempalace_kg_query`/timeline, and writes a
examples: gsd-mempalace-recall          # Recall for the current phase
gsd-mempalace-recall 03-auth  # Recall scoped to a specific phase slug

name: milestone-summary
description: Generate comprehensive project summary from milestone artifacts for team onboarding and review.

name: mvp-phase
description: Guided MVP planning for a phase — prompts for a user story, runs SPIDR splitting check, writes `**Mode:** mvp` to ROADMAP.md, then delegates to `/gsd-plan-phase` (which auto-detects MVP mode via the roadmap field).
examples: gsd-mvp-phase 2.1                  # MVP planning for a decimal phase
gsd-mvp-phase 3 --force            # Convert phase 3 even if in-progress

name: new-milestone
description: Start next version cycle.
examples: gsd-new-milestone --reset-phase-numbers "v2.0 Mobile"  # Restart milestone numbering at 1

name: new-project
description: Initialize a new project with deep context gathering.

flags.description: Auto-extract from document, skip interactive questions

name: onboard
description: Guide an existing codebase through first-time GSD onboarding. The command checks repo state, routes you through codebase mapping, optional docs ingest, project initialization, and creates an onboarding summary once planning exists.

flags.description: Prefer the lightweight `/gsd-map-codebase --fast` mapping handoff; a complete map is still required before `/gsd-new-project`
Use numbered plain-text gates instead of TUI menus

examples: gsd-onboard --fast    # Use lightweight codebase mapping first, then complete the map before project setup

name: pause-work
description: Save context handoff when stopping mid-phase.

flags.description: Generate a post-session summary in `.planning/reports/` capturing commits, file changes, and phase progress

name: phase
description: CRUD for phases in ROADMAP.md — add, insert, remove, or edit phases with a single consolidated command.

flags.description: Insert urgent work as a decimal phase (e.g., 3.1) after phase N
Remove a future phase and renumber subsequent phases
Edit any field of an existing phase in place
Allow editing in-progress or completed phases (used with `--edit`)

examples: gsd-phase "Add authentication system"          # Append new phase with description
gsd-phase --insert 3 "Fix auth race condition" # Insert between phase 3 and 4 → creates 3.1
gsd-phase --edit 5                 # Edit any field of phase 5
gsd-phase --edit 5 --force         # Edit phase 5 even if in-progress or completed

name: plan-phase
description: Research, plan, and verify a phase.

flags.description: Force re-research even if RESEARCH.md exists
Research-only mode: spawn researcher for phase `<N>`, write RESEARCH.md, exit before planner. Supersedes the deleted standalone research command (#3042).
Research-only modifier: when used with `--research-phase`, print existing RESEARCH.md to stdout and exit (no spawn).
Gap closure mode (reads VERIFICATION.md, skips research)
Use a PRD file instead of discuss-phase for context
Use ADR file(s) instead of discuss-phase for context synthesis
Optional ADR parser format override for `--ingest`
Replan with cross-AI review feedback from REVIEWS.md
Run state validation before planning begins
Run external plan bounce validation after planning (uses `workflow.plan_bounce_script`)
Skip plan bounce even if enabled in config

Vertical MVP mode — planner organizes tasks as feature slices (UI→API→DB) instead of horizontal layers. On Phase 1 of a new project with no prior phase summaries, also emits `SKELETON.md` (Walking Skeleton). Can be persisted on a phase via `**Mode:** mvp` in ROADMAP.md, which applies `--mvp` automatically without the flag.

TDD mode — planner applies `type: tdd` to eligible behavior-adding tasks so each begins with a failing test. Composable with `--mvp`: `--mvp --tdd` produces vertical slices where every behavior-adding task starts red-green.

Override the planning granularity for this invocation, ignoring config. Valid values: `coarse`, `standard`, `fine`. Takes precedence over `granularities.planning`, top-level `granularity`, and `planning.granularity` config.

examples: gsd-plan-phase 2 --ingest docs/adr/0010.md   # ADR express path for context synthesis
gsd-plan-phase --research-phase 4             # Research only on phase 4 (auto-uses existing RESEARCH.md, no prompt)

name: plan-review-convergence
description: Cross-AI plan convergence loop — replan with review feedback until no HIGH concerns remain and no actionable MEDIUM/LOW findings remain outside `PLAN.md`. Runs `plan-phase → review → replan → re-review` cycles (max 3 cycles by default). Pla

name: pr-branch
description: Create a clean PR branch by filtering out `.planning/` commits.

name: profile-user
description: Generate a developer behavioral profile from Claude Code session analysis across 8 dimensions (communication style, decision patterns, debugging approach, UX preferences, vendor choices, frustration triggers, learning style, explanation dep

flags.description: Use interactive questionnaire instead of session analysis

examples: gsd-profile-user                   # Analyze sessions and build profile

name: progress
description: Show status, next steps, and automatically advance to the next logical workflow step. Reads project state and determines the appropriate action. Use `/gsd:next` when you want an interactive smart-entry menu before dispatch; use `/gsd-progre

flags.description: Automatically advance to the next logical workflow step without manual route selection
Like `--next`, but chains steps automatically until milestone completion or a blocking decision
When the next action is planning, route it through `/gsd-plan-review-convergence`; requires `workflow.plan_review_convergence=true`
Analyze freeform intent and dispatch to the most appropriate GSD command
Append a 6-check integrity audit after the standard report (STATE consistency, orphaned handoffs, deferred scope drift, memory-flagged pending work, blocking todos, uncommitted code)

examples: gsd-progress                       # "Where am I? What's next?" with auto-routing
gsd-progress --next                # Advance to next step automatically
gsd-progress --next --auto         # Chain steps automatically until completion
gsd-progress --next --auto --converge  # Hands-free run with plan-review convergence
gsd-progress --do "fix the auth bug"  # Dispatch freeform intent to best GSD command

name: project
description: Project lifecycle router — milestones, audits, summary

name: quality
description: Quality gates router — code review, debug, audit, security, eval, ui

name: quick
description: Execute ad-hoc task with GSD guarantees.

flags.description: Enable the complete quality pipeline — discussion + research + plan-checking + verification
Plan-checking (max 2 iterations) + post-execution verification only; no discussion or research
List all quick tasks with status
Show status of a specific quick task
Resume a specific quick task by slug

examples: gsd-quick list                     # List all quick tasks
gsd-quick status my-task-slug      # Show status of a quick task
gsd-quick resume my-task-slug      # Resume a quick task

name: resume-work
description: Restore full context from last session.
examples: gsd-resume-work                    # After context reset or new session

name: review
description: Cross-AI peer review of phase plans from external AI CLIs.

flags.description: Include Qwen Code review (Alibaba Qwen models)
Include all available reviewers (CLI + local model servers)

name: review-backlog
description: Review and promote backlog items to active milestone.

name: secure-phase
description: Retroactively verify threat mitigations for a completed phase.

name: settings
description: Interactive configuration of workflow toggles and model profile. Questions are grouped into six visual sections:

name: ship
description: Create PR from completed phase work with auto-generated body.

name: sketch
description: Explore design directions through throwaway HTML mockups before committing to implementation. Produces 2–3 variants per design question for direct browser comparison.
examples: gsd-sketch --wrap-up                   # Package winning sketch into a skill

name: spec-phase
description: Clarify WHAT a phase delivers through Socratic questioning with quantitative ambiguity scoring, then probe for omitted edges. Produces `SPEC.md` before discuss-phase.

flags.description: Skip interactive questions; Claude selects recommended defaults and writes SPEC.md
Use plain-text numbered lists instead of TUI menus (required for `/rc` remote sessions)

examples: gsd-spec-phase 1                  # Interactive spec + edge probe for phase 1
gsd-spec-phase 3 --auto           # Auto-select defaults; never auto-dismisses an edge
gsd-spec-phase 2 --text           # Plain-text menus for remote sessions

name: spike
description: Run 2–5 focused feasibility experiments before committing to an implementation approach. Each experiment uses Given/When/Then framing, produces executable code, and returns a VALIDATED / INVALIDATED / PARTIAL verdict.
examples: gsd-spike "can we stream LLM tokens through SSE"
gsd-spike --wrap-up                    # Package findings into a reusable skill

name: stats
description: Display project statistics.

name: surface
description: Toggle which skills are surfaced — apply a profile, list, or disable a cluster without reinstall.

flags.description: Show enabled and disabled clusters and skills
Alias for `list` plus token cost summary
Add cluster to disabled list and re-stage
Remove cluster from disabled list and re-stage
Delete surface delta; return to install-time profile
Resolve, verify, consent-gate, and install a capability from a registry / git / npm / tarball / local source
Re-resolve a capability's recorded source and upgrade it (atomic stage-then-swap)
Remove an installed overlay capability's files + marker-isolated shared edits (first-party cannot be removed here)
List first-party + installed overlay capabilities as a JSON array

Light-peek each installed overlay's recorded source and report which have a newer version available (per-source matrix; npm ranges resolve the highest matching version; `pinned` for immutable/explicit git refs or exact npm versions; `manual`/`unknown` for sources that can't be auto-checked)

examples: gsd-surface profile standard       # Switch to standard profile
gsd-surface disable utility        # Disable the utility cluster

name: thread
description: Manage persistent context threads for cross-session work.

flags.description: Dry-run mode — show proposed changes without writing
Compute the rebuild and emit a structured preview, write nothing
Tee the audit-log entries to stderr in addition to writing them to STATE.md
Enforce Conventional Commits format on git commit messages

examples: gsd-thread list --open             # List only open/in-progress threads
gsd-thread list --resolved         # List only resolved threads
gsd-thread status fix-deploy-key   # Show thread status
gsd-thread close fix-deploy-key    # Mark thread as resolved
gsd-thread "Investigate TCP timeout in pasta service"  # Create new

name: ui-phase
description: Generate UI design contract for frontend phases.

name: ui-review
description: Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend.

name: ultraplan-phase
description: /gsd-ultraplan-phase 4                  # Offload planning for phase 4

name: undo
description: Safe git revert — roll back GSD phase or plan commits using the phase manifest with dependency checks and a confirmation gate.
examples: gsd-undo --last 5                  # Pick from the 5 most recent GSD commits
gsd-undo --phase 03                # Revert all commits for phase 3
gsd-undo --plan 03-02              # Revert commits for plan 02 of phase 3

name: update
description: Update GSD with changelog preview, and optionally sync skills or reapply local patches.

flags.description: Sync skills from the GSD registry after updating

examples: gsd-update                         # Check for updates and install
gsd-update --reapply               # Update and reapply local patches
gsd-update --next                  # Install from the @next RC dist-tag

name: validate-phase
description: Retroactively audit and fill Nyquist validation gaps.
examples: gsd-validate-phase 2               # Audit test coverage for phase 2

name: verify-work
description: User acceptance testing with auto-diagnosis.

flags.description: Stable identifier (`D1`, `D2`…), unique within the SUMMARY
Test path + descriptor, screenshot ref, or command
Required boolean. `true` always routes to a human

name: workflow
description: Phase pipeline router — discuss, plan, execute, verify, phase, progress, next

name: workspace
description: Manage GSD workspaces — create, list, or remove isolated workspace environments with repo copies and independent `.planning/` directories.

flags.description: Create a new workspace (use with `--name`, `--repos`, etc.)
List active GSD workspaces and their status
Remove a workspace and clean up git worktrees
Comma-separated repo paths or names (used with `--new`)

name: workstreams
description: Manage parallel workstreams for concurrent work on different milestone areas.

flags.description: List all workstreams with status (default if no subcommand)

examples: gsd-workstreams create backend-api # Create new workstream
gsd-workstreams switch backend-api # Set active workstream
gsd-workstreams complete backend-api  # Archive completed workstream
gsd-workstreams resume backend-api    # Resume work in workstream
